etymology.description: 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder") + 紅(紅) (hóng, "red") = powdery red -> pink
etymology.interpretation: The term 粉紅(fěnhóng) derives its meaning from combining 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder"), which evokes a sense of lightness and dilution, with 紅(紅) (hóng, "red"), which provides the base color. The combination suggests a red that has been lightened or softened into a pink hue, akin to the appearance of red when mixed with white powder.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢粉红色的花(我喜歡粉紅色的花)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān fěnhóng sè de huā.

examples.chinese: 她穿了一件粉红裙子(她穿了一件粉紅裙子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuānle yī jiàn fěnhóng qúnzi.

examples.chinese: 这面墙是粉红色的(這面牆是粉紅色的)，感觉很温暖(感覺很溫暖)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè miàn qiáng shì fěnhóng sè de, gǎnjué hěn wēnnuǎn.
examples.english: This wall is pink, and it feels very warm.
